name: homekit-automator
description: Smart HomeKit automation with natural language — control devices, create automations registered as Apple Shortcuts, get energy insights and suggestions. Multi-home support, full validation pipeline, condition evaluation, and structured logging.
author: homekit-automator

mcpServers.name: homekit-automator
mcpServers.description: HomeKit device control and automation via MCP

homekitauto.description: HomeKit Automator CLI — device control, automation management, and home intelligence
homekitauto.installInstructions: Install via Homebrew: brew install homekit-automator/tap/homekit-automator. Or build from source: cd scripts/swift && swift build -c release, then copy .build/release/homekitauto to /usr/local/bin/

features: Full HomeKit device discovery and control (lights, thermostats, locks, doors, fans, sensors, etc.)
Automation engine with cron, solar, manual, and device-state triggers
Apple Shortcuts registration with existence check before import
Full validation pipeline: device existence with Levenshtein fuzzy match, characteristic support/writability, value ranges, cron parsing
Condition evaluation at runtime during automation test
Multi-home support with --home flag threaded through all commands
Enhanced suggestion engine with seasonal and pattern-based suggestions
Device state monitoring (state_changes, subscribe socket commands)
SwiftUI menu bar app with dashboard, settings, and history views
50 Swift unit tests, 14 MCP integration tests, 24 evals

1.1.0: Fixed automation edit to decode and apply new actions/triggers
Renamed --json to --definition for automation create inline JSON
Added shortcut existence check before import
Added temperature F↔C conversion with --units option
Full validation pipeline (device existence with Levenshtein fuzzy match, characteristic support/writability, value ranges, cron parsing)
Duplicate name enforcement in automation registry
Condition evaluation at runtime in automation test
Enhanced suggestion engine with seasonal + pattern-based suggestions
Multi-home support (--home threaded through all commands)
Device state monitoring (state_changes, subscribe socket commands)
50 Swift tests, 14 MCP integration tests, 24 evals
Full SwiftUI menu bar app (dashboard, settings, history, helper lifecycle)
1.0.0: Initial release with core HomeKit bridge, CLI, MCP server, automation engine, and Shortcuts integration
